title: Instrument de ratification britannique de l'accord et du protocole en vue de préciser les conditions dans lesquelles devront être exécutées certaines clauses du Traité

pages.text:
George, be the Grace of God, of the

United kingdom of Great Britain and freland and of the Iritish

Dominions beyond the Seas ling,

Defender of the faith, Emperor of 3ndía, &c., Sc., Sc. To all ano singular to wbom tbese Presents shall come, Greeting !

Tubereas a Treaty between Us and the

Powers and States therein specified, providing for the termination of the State of War existing between certain of the Allied and Associated Powers of the one part and Bulgaria of the other part, was concluded and signed at Neuilly-sur-Seine on the Twenty-Seventh day of November, in the year of Our Lord One

Thousand Nine Hundred and Nineteen, by the Pleni- potentiaries of Us and of the Allied and Associated

Powers and Bulgaria, duly and respectively authorized for that purposc, together with a Protocol indicating the conditions in which certain provisions of the

Treaty are to be carried out, which Treaty and

Protocol are, word for word, as follows:—

Vile, having seen and considered the Treaty and Protocol aforesaid, have approved, accepted, and confirmed the same in all and every one of their

Articles and Clauses, as We do by these Presents approve, accept, confirm, and ratify them for Ourselves,

Our Heirs and Successors; engaging and promising upon Our Royal Word that We will sincerely and faithfully perform and observe all and singular the things which are contained and expressed in the

Treaty and Protocol aforesaid, and that We will never suffer the same to be violated by any one, or transgressed in any manner, as far as it lies in Our power. For the greater testimony and validity of all which, We have caused Our Great Seal to be affixed to these Presents, which We have signed with Our

Royal Hand.

Given at Our Court of St. James, the thirtieth day of April, in the year of Our Lord

One Thousand Nine Hundred and Twenty, and in the Tenth year of Our Reign.

GeorgeR. 8
